reviews.review_msg: This course was almost completely taught through the mandatory pre lecture videos that were assigned before each class. The actual lectures were mostly spent going over specific examples and no actual explaining or teaching seemed to happen. Tests were extremely difficult, but they would curve if everyone did poorly.

Lectures were organized and straightforward. She would often do clicker questions and go over examples which was helpful. Tests are far more difficult than the homework and practice tests but she scales marks pretty generously.

DO THE HOMEWORK. Her tests are easy if you do the homework and fully understand it. She loves helping students so go to office hours and I find what she says in office hours is more easy to understand from what she says during lectures. If you can't meet her during her office hours she also answers questions on canvas.
She is very easy to talk to, she will help you with your questions outside of class time, and has many office hours. She is good at explaining problems.
Really hard to sit through lectures, her explanations weren't easy to understand. Midterms and finals are reasonable. def wont take anything with her again if possible.

Dr. Johnson is a really good teacher. There is a lot of HW though, but I think that's every physics class. Just try not to skip lectures, because she uses iclicker polls in class. Course load is pretty heavy though so be prepared. Dr. Johnson is pretty chill though, she'll help with any question you have.

The homework is tough, and do expect to spend hours on a single question, but as long as you like physics it is all good practice (I say this with a 65%). Dr. Johnson gets straight to the point. She has a discussion page where her students can ask her anything and she responds to all of them in a timely manner.
she sounds like she's crying

Her lectures were not helpful, she doesnt engage with the class as much as other profs do. An ungodly amount of homework, and do not miss lectures or tutorials or else you will lose a grade because of iClicker and Tutorial Attendances. Hard to reach outside class and very condescending, Overall, just strengthened my distaste for physics even more.

This class has an unreasonable workload. There are 4 mandatory classes/tutorials to show up to every week. There are 2 prelectures which are not too bad to complete. However every week there are 9-12 assignment questions. They are very difficult and no similar questions to what appears on the assignments are covered in class. Plus a tut question.

I thought it was my problem or I was bad at physics, but the fact is that, finally I realized many of people around me have taken this class at least once. And they all had really bad experience of this course. Don't take it unless you have to! If you have to... well, good luck.
Karen was extremely kind and was caring enough to help profusely with the homework and tests, and the entire class revolves around knowing Newton's laws. Best Teacher EVER
bad professor, fried my brain and made me never want to do physics again,

This was genuinely the worst class I've ever taken. The prelectures are the worst things ever because they explain the topics using methods we were told not to use. And if you don't understand the prelecture you're screwed cause the lecture is just Karen going over the prelecture questions and not actually explaining any of the topics. DONT TAKE IT
Karen was pretty bad. Her lectures were bad, and she was very unorganized.

Lectures were pointless because she re-explains the pre-lectures in fancy wording to make her sound smarter than she is, even though she was learning the course material with us. Midterms were difficult as it was testing how well you can read instead of actual physics, homework had nothing similar to exams either. Final was brutal. Good luck lol

Avoid this prof at all costs. She is so bad at teaching Physics. Before each lecture, we had to complete lots of pre-lectures, which the videos sucks and yet she goes over them in class in a worse way, instead of teaching. Her midterms were easy but the final, so bad to the point I never wanted to take physics again. What a waste of tuition!

karen has actually made me so miserable to the point where after coming from my physics final at 12 AM i never want to take physics. if i knew about phys 125 before enrolling i would have taken that class at any chance given that karen was teaching phys 120 i would prefer the harder overall course than any  course taught by karen. run for your life
what the hell was that final. I got an A on both midterms and genuinely might have failed that stupid test. what the hel

What a waste of tuition. Karen is by far one of the worst people you can ever get as person to teach you physics. Prelectures are mandatory and so are her Iclickers. They are both worth together 15%.
We told her that the Midterms were difficult, and yet made the final harder than PHYS125. 
What a shame to call her a Dr. & Professor. SMH.

Makes students watch prelectures that covered everything that will happen in the lecture, but during the lecture, she makes topics more confusing. She also does mandatory iclickers which count for marks. Besides for the confusing lectures and heavy prelecture focus, she also makes test extremely hard.

Is a nice person but that's really it. 

If you aren't good at keeping up with deadlines, don't take her class, if you're ok with a more spread out grading/assignments then it might be for you. 

Lectures are attendance mandatory but she just repeats what's already done in prelecture, making it feel like a waste of tuition.

she is nice but so bad a teaching. you'd think by paying money to come to school you wouldn't have to self teach but boy are you wrong. she just repeats the achieve videos is a worse way and videos themselves are terrible. the way the teacher in the videos talks make me want to end myself its so annoying. made me lose any interest I had in physics

Karen is so nice. But experiencing her as a teacher has made me crash out. There's an absurd amount of homework, concepts are taught entirely by mass produced videos from Macmillan, lectures are useless and confusing. But attendance is somehow mandatory... this class has taught me nothing.
Just no need to make lecture and tutorial mandatory in a class like this. She focuses solely on pre-lecture videos making lecture basically useless but mandatory for grades through Iclicker.

She cannot teach for the life of her. The pre lectures are the main source of learning and in lecture she repeats the same things in a more confusing way. Attendance is part of your grade. Tests are fair and easier than homework. If you self teach you will be okay. I stopped going to class and just bit the bullet cause it helped me in the long run.

This prof cannot teach at all. Her explanations make absolutely no sense whatsoever, leaving people more and more confused. I-clickers make up 80% of the lectures, with little to none have resemblance to exams. Not just that, written and achieve homeworks were so dreadful, taking days to complete. However, the tests are somewhat on the easier side.
karen i am begging you to never teach another physics course. you cannot please i am so depressed 😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭😭

i want to end myself because of her awful explanations. she makes me not like living. like actually i am miserable in this course when she just tells me simplest parts about physics and not going into detail about the things i am asking to the point where she waits 20 minutes expecting me to get it when i have no fing idea

Karen is possibly the worst teacher/professor I have had for Physics. Not only do I have no clue what I am supposed to be doing for some of the homework, the parts I go to her for help she will only explain it in enigmas to the point where she will ask questions that are simple and leave me puzzled on the stuff I need to know. Avoid for sanity.

You have to buy a 100$ textbook that sucks, and she has in-class questions that don't help with understanding the subject. The pre-lectures and bridge questions before class make the subject more complicated, and her lectures just go over those questions instead of actually teaching stuff. The only upside is that her tests aren't very hard.

Dr. Kavanagh is great as a person but horrible as a lecturer. Going into the lectures leaves people more confused, as she is horrid at teaching important concepts. So many iClicker questions for marks, making attendance mandatory. Homeworks are not rated to lectures and takes hours or even days to complete. Tests are also hard so good luck.

DO NOT TAKE her course unless you want to teach yourself everything. She's not a bad person but she is absolutely horrid at teaching. She just repeats the achieve videos that you're supposed to watch yourself so she's literally not even the one teaching you. The test are nothing like any of the practice and the homework you get will take you hours.

Avoid avoid avoid. You are required to watch an hours worth of videos before every lecture, and all she does is repeat those videos while making it more confusing. Tests are similar to practice exams but you wont understand those if you don't self study at least a bit. So many iClicker questions make lectures more about those than learning.

Genuinely did not learn anything from attending lectures yet they are mandatory since she does iClicker questions. All the information you can get is from textbook and prelectures... She does reply to emails quickly and exams are on the *slightly* easier side, nice person overall just a bad prof

She can't teach. Lectures are awful. The ratings and comments made by others are accurate do not take the course if she's the teacher. Unable to solve her own problems in class. Prelectures are more useful than lectures homework questions are extremely hard compared to lecture questions. Even ChatGPT not saving you
Very confusing lectures. Reads off of her slides. Tests are nowhere near the practice material provided for it. Tons of homework, I-Clicker is necessary. Take this class with someone else.

Awesome prof! Aside from the fact that office hours overlapped with the PHYS 132 lab time a little he is very friendly and helpful during office hours and after class. Class is structured to be more problem-solving oriented so be sure to pay attention to the pre-lecture videos (or read the textbook if you prefer that).
I really liked Dr. H, i had fun attending his lecture. His midterms were fine, but final was really hard. I would give a 5 but the final hit my gpa:(. He seems like a really smart guy too.
Highinbottom is really funny and great at keeping your attention, he seems to be really passionate about physics.

Very nice prof. He clearly shows a lot of passion for teaching physics. However I feel the required prelectures take away from that, as his lectures are just a restatement. IClickers are mandatory for attendance which is annoying. Not a bad class at all, I feel it just needs to be structured different.
Struggles to explain anything during lecture, also do not sit in the first few roads his breath smells awful. Somebody tell this physics prof to go brush his teeth my god it smells like the worst 🤢

Took it last semester, failed both midterms and studied like my life depended on it only to clutch the final everyone failed, other classes feel easy now. This man will help you dig deep into the abyss that is your soul to find potential that hasn't seen the light of day, whether you like it or not.
He's a helpful teacher, gives lots example in the lecture and try to let u understand the concept.
No Comments
Boring, repetitive, and rather unorganized . . . his tests also killed.
do not take a class with him ever. Doesn't teach properly, exams are nothing like homework, take with any other prof even if u have to wait a semester.
